query: Minnesota Finlandia cross country ski race Bemidji

videos.title: Common Ground 422 Minnesota Finlandia
videos.description: Join Common Ground as we suit up for the Minnesota Finlandia cross-country ski marathon in Bemidji. We'll show you what makes this race one of the most prestigious and challenging events in the world.
videos.transcript:
Lakeland Public television's common
ground is brought to you by the
Minnesota arts and cultural heritage
fund this is the Premier event in the
greater bidi area the Minnesota fandia
ski Marathon we have a variety of events
people can do we've got a 50k skating
race we've got a 25k skating race a 25k
classical race and a really unique race
called The Pursuit where they ski half
of the race on their classical skis and
the other half on their skate skis
my name is Mark Walters I'm the Nordic
ski coach at the piji high school and
I've been doing this for about 10 years
and I'm also the trail administrator for
all of what you see out here we are here
for our biggest race of the Season
locally which is the Minnesota Finlandia
ski Marathon I have lots of high school
skiers here past High School skiers we
have a variety of events to choose from
you can do the 25k classic you can do a
25 km free Style skating style event we
have a combination event called The
Pursuit where we're going to Classic the
East half of bav Vista and then skate
the West half and then there's the main
event which is the 50k freestyle
Marathon right now we're at BO Vista Ski
Area we're standing on the Cross Country
Trails at buav Vista Ski Area that uh
the Minnesota Finlandia is is held out
every February uh around President Day
the Minnesota Finlandia is the second
oldest ski race in Minnesota and it was
one of the first what's now the American
ski Marathon series which is a series of
races throughout the country in Alaska
Colorado um Wisconsin uh which is the
babiner and others out in the east coast
we are part of that Series in which
there's 12 different events throughout
the winter and we're one of the founding
members of that American ski Marathon
series my goal today is to do a 25 km
classical ski race and I'm going to use
that as a warm-up to an event coming up
next weekend which is a big classical
ski race for me um the 25k classic is um
simply running the entire east side of
the bav Vista Trail system back through
this transition area through the tunnel
and then the big hilly west side and
back on
in no I've never skied the Minnesota
Finlandia I have skied the course many
of times but uh I have just never had
the time to do the Finlandia my name is
Mark morsy I'm the chief of course for
Finlandia I do a lot of the trail
grooming out here getting the course
ready and we try to make it a really
nice experience for everybody from the
racers to the 10K tour for the first
time uh I'm going to ski around try to
get with some skiers and kind of get a
point of view uh and take some little
video of techniques that the skiers use
kind of show you around the
course yeah thanks
uh we got a lot more snow than we've had
and so we're having a lot of fun with it
this year we had drifts like we're out
in Yellowstone so we had to work the
snow down to get it fast that's the way
the skiers like it skiers like to go
fast so it's a joy to work with this
organization we're having a lot of funi
racers hey volunteers wave to me
volunteers wave to me wave to
me the suitcas folks thr into the
transition area coming out with their
skate skis on
we have uh five different events we have
the Minnesota Finlandia 50k Marathon
which was the original race um we have
the 25k classic we have a 25k skate or
freestyle and a 25k Pursuit continuous
Pursuit which is uh a leg of classic and
a and a leg of skate um and the
Northwoods tour the Northwoods tour is a
10K tour it doesn't go the full course
it just gives people an introduction as
to what the trails are looking like they
get to participate in the feed stations
and uh go through the finish line for a
lot of people the beginning of this race
can be a little intimidating if you look
in the background there's the huge climb
up Sunnys side which gets you over the
top of the continental divide the east
side though is is very rolling and
comfortable terrain to ski once you go
through the tunnel and enter the West
there's some big climbs some very
technical descents and that is a
challenge for a lot of people for a
classical skier like myself his it's a
matter of hitting the kick wax correct
so that can stay in the classical track
and not slip and slide all around the
course is very challenging that's one
thing that we continually hear from
skiers from all over the world really
it's a challenging course it's also very
beautiful and very Scenic one of the
more challenging Parts is skiers at one
point right out of the start have to ski
up the downhill area that Bo of fista
offers it's also a downhill area and
skiers within the first K are heading
straight up the hill you know we're on a
very historic piece of ground here owned
by the Dickinson and this whole bav
Vista piece of land behind us again is
the Continental Divide which actually
has Flowage that goes towards the north
into the whole Hudson Winnipeg is kind
of region and then into the Mississippi
Watershed it's huge up there then that's
it's one of the biggest hills here for
downhill skiing yet we have to go up it
again it it's a huge Challenge and for
the people that go around this course
twice the thought of around and hitting
that climb again is pretty intimidating
people just have to learn to Pace
themselves going up it because it it
takes about 2 2 and 1/2 minutes to get
up it the pursuit race is something new
within the past six years we kind of
found a little niche or a need for
something different and uh we're one of
the only Marathon series that has the
pursuit and what it entails is they
start with the 25k classic they do 14k
on their classic skis then they come in
and transition on the Fly and then they
go and skate 12K on the other side of
the course and then finish in the same
fashion so it's a 25k all together the
two different styles of skiing that uh
people do at the Finlandia are number
one classic skiing which is basically
the original type of skiing I believe
it's the Norwegians that invented it but
it's basically shushing your feet back
and forth in a track that we put in the
snow and they've got what's called a
kick Zone in the middle of their skis
that are able B to grip so you grip and
then you Glide the other style is
freestyle or skate in which it's very
similar to ice skating but you got you
know longer skis it's the faster way to
get around the course so skate we send
out later on besides the classic the
classic takes a little bit longer so
they go out earlier traditional
classical skiing is the foundation of
Nordic skiing it's it's what most people
think about it's where we have Glide wax
on the tip and the tail of the ski and
then we're putting a hard wax in the
center to give us kick we need we need
to grab the snow so we can stride
forward okay skating came around in the
very late 1980s and pretty much
dominated Nordic skiing for a good 10 or
15 years it's just Glide wax from tip to
tail it simulates maybe inline skating
more than anything else and there's no
kick involved okay it's just pure Glide
and it's a very aggressive style of ski
if an athlete is well-versed in both the
skating will be about 10% quicker skate
skiing actually evolves and became a
discovery it it actually was a little
bit of a piece of classical skiing where
we started keeping one of our skis in
the track and then pushing off with the
other classical ski which we refer to as
the marathon skate and then of course it
just evolved into well let's just skate
okay and it's really changed a lot in
the way we groom how we maintain our ski
trails you know we're always manicuring
them really flat so we have a nice fast
fast course what I think makes this race
so great is uh the snow conditions that
we have
every year we're faced with really
interesting challenges with the snow but
we always seem to pull together a
fantastic course I've got a great crew
that goes out and grooms this stuff up
and the thing I hear the most from
anybody coming in from either the cities
or Canada is they can't believe what the
trails are really like they they just
don't believe it and when they get here
they're stuck and they keep coming back
every year unlike a lot of ski races
because we are a la course meaning if
you're doing the 50 km event you go
around twice there's a handful of spots
that a spectator can not only just watch
the start and the Finish but they get to
watch transition zones in the pursuit
the skiers getting out of their classic
ski jumping into their skate ski we've
got a lap Lane where the people are
coming through that are going around for
their second time so visually and
spectator Wise It's very friendly as
well I think one of the more interesting
pieces to the Minnesota Finlandia would
be the people the volunteers are so
gracious to spend their day out in the
cold either at the feed stations uh
we've got a wonderful Hall of Fame feed
with tons of food um that's another
thing that we hear a lot of uh people
complimenting on is is all this
wonderful homemade free food um and I
think the third would be the course I
think the course kind of speaks for
itself it's a challenging course but
it's also a gratifying course uh when
you finish you feel like you we've
definitely done something the biggest
draw to cross country skiing is it's a
lifestyle okay this is a very healthy
sport it's something that as long as you
can walk you can cross country ski it's
why I enjoy coaching My Philosophy is
I'm giving you a sport that Beyond high
school and Beyond College you can
continue to do this I ski in huge
marathons next to gentlemen and ladies
that are in their 70s ' 80s '90s you
know and they're still skiing strong in
Europe it's it's not uncommon to have
hundreds of people in their 70s in a big
Marathon well a good way to get started
is check into a local ski club if you
have one in your community sometimes
Community Ed programs have Nordic ski
lessons just hook up with some folks
that have knowledge take a few lessons
like anything else getting on the right
start is the way to progress with it
makes a lot less frustrating and it gets
you outside in the
winter we typically start uh getting
going in about October or November and
start getting our Flyers out um if
you're interested in participating we do
have online registration and we mail out
registrations in the fall to all past
participants and encourage them to
invite their friends and we do a lot of
advertising a lot now with the internet
we've reached a lot more people
worldwide uh we do have two Norwegians
in here in the race this year that uh
they were pretty excited to come in in
ski the Finlandia they've heard from it
all the way over in in Norway for
volunteers to get involved they can
email us or contact uh buav Vista to get
involved we're always looking for new
blood board members volunteers what
people should really come to understand
about the Minnesota fandia ski race is
that it is probably one of the most
relaxed friendly ski marathons that you
can come to if you look around you can
park right here at the start you can ski
your race you can come back you can get
into your nice warm vehicle we do the
Wards right on site we're not shuttling
hundreds of thousands of people in buses
back and forth this course can easily
handle 5 or 600 folks in this exact
format and it's a beautiful beautiful
course and it's it's a wonderful warmup
to like the American babiner coming up
next weekend and things like that
so if you enjoyed this segment of

videos.transcript:
a buena vista ski resort hosted the 39th
annual finlandia ski marathon on
saturday with 124 competitors spread out
among five different races the marathon
saw almost every competitor finish mary
balstad has more in this week's
northwoods adventure
starting in 1978 at the bemidji
waterfront a group of skiers took two
days to travel from the paul and babe
statue to buena vista ski resort and
back to the bemidji stadium
now more than 40 years later the
marathon has grown to include more than
100 ski enthusiasts they join in the
single day race to take part in a
historic event that has connections with
the finnish consulate when it was
started back in 1978
some of the organizers contacted the
finnish consulate
there was i guess some connections at
that point and
they gave us permission to call it the
minnesota finlandia
and
when they did that
we were sponsored by a finnish consulate
finlandia vodka a
whole bunch of finished things
and kept it ever since recognized
nationwide the finlandia ski marathon
holds the title of best course and best
overall ski event by skiers of the
minnesota skinny ski series falling
between two other major ski marathons in
the midwest minnesota finlandia often
draws in a large crowd sometimes with
international fame we've had
olympic skiers from pretty much all over
the world ski this thing at one point
you know we've we've had
bill cox ski this he was an olympic
medalist
we we get so far this year what i know
we have is is we have some from illinois
we have some from colorado
typically we get a pretty decent
contingent from canada but this year
with covid they're not making it down
but yeah we get we get skiers from all
around i remember about 10 years ago we
had three
skiers that came from russia they they
couldn't even speak english and they did
our race weather related issues and the
pandemic caused cancellations in the
past however thanks to a well-planned
schedule and tenacious participants that
kept the finish line in sight the 39th
finlandia ski marathon came to a close
reporting from bemidji mary balston
lakeland news no award ceremony took
place due to the ongoing covet 19
pandemic top male and female racers did
receive recognition and a handmade
wooden axe decoration
lakeland news is member supported
content please consider supporting
lakeland news today

videos.title: Minnesota Finlandia Promo Video
videos.description:
Visit http://www.minnesotafinlandia.com/ for more info!

The Minnesota Finlandia is a cross country ski marathon held every year in February in northern Minnesota, near Bemidji. The race features am expertly groomed rolling course, excellent aid stations, post race food, the best prizes ever, and more!

We'll see you in 2015!

videos.transcript:
skiers made their way over the hill and
across the trails at the 40th annual
Finlandia skiing event held at Buena
Vista Ski Resort North of Bemidji the
event saw over 100 participants go the
extra kilometer to celebrate this
Milestone here's this week's Northwoods
experience
[Music]
of different ages and experiences took
to the trails for the 40th Finlandia ski
event this past weekend there's a lot of
big ski marathons all over the state in
the Midwest and a lot of them haven't
been around nearly as long as the
Finlandia we've got a bunch of people
from Canada coming down some people from
the Twin Cities coming up Fargo but from
all over the place it's good to see that
the community support something like
this for 40 some years
whether it was for the first place
finish or just a personal best time
faces new and old looked to celebrate
this Milestone it just shows how much
the how much this race means to people
how much the organizers have put it put
effort into it every year it's an all
ages event it's a great chance to kind
of test your skills and it's got some of
the best prizes in all of cross-country
skiing well recognizing the
accomplishments of 40 Years of Finlandia
organizers managed to work around
challenges on and off the ski Trail
including the drop in participation
numbers races like this there's always
going to be fluctuations a couple of
years that are down couple years that
are high so participation has dropped
over the years only because you know
there's more and more races all around
the state today alone I think there's
five different races within the Midwest
here that people can choose from
members this year this year participants
who cross that finish line now look to
the Future for Finlandia in the future
we'd like to see the numbers go back up
again so you know if we could do you
know a little more cross you know a
promotion and things like that and
organize with other races and trying to
get their numbers back up but other than
that I mean we're really happy with the
way things turn out

videos.title: Northwoods Adventure: 36th Annual Minnesota Finlandia
videos.transcript:
well it's a tradition that spans 36
years and attracts skiers from all over
the region
and this week's North Woods adventurer
Josh Peterson takes a look at why the
events deep traditions continue to bring
people back after all these years after
months of preparation the horn sounded
and skiers from all over the region
glided their way across the countryside
of the Buena Vista forests the Minnesota
Finlandia has been a winter tradition in
the Bemidji area for 36 years and this
year was extra special because it was
the first time in four years skiers got
to enjoy the full course the racers
preferred to do the full 25k because the
east side is more gradual rolling and it
gives them a chance to take a break and
then rest up before they hit the real
heavy side besides its deep history and
rich traditions what draws people to the
Minnesota Finlandia is the terrain and
beauty of the course beauty mixed with
tradition is one of the reasons that
many of those involved with the race
keep coming back year after year the
course is great and we almost always
have for some cross-country skiing it's
a family sport for the Ehlert's ins from
Albert Lea the Minnesota Finlandia
provided father and son a chance to
compete together while mom enjoys the
trip to the North Country cheering her
family on to victory
keeping the passion for the sport with
his dad so it's really fun to see them
ski together
and support the sport we love you know
gets gets you through Minnesota winters
having having a hobby like this and a
passion like this they train all year
round together for those competing the
full course is an enjoyable challenge
and we'll be back next year to defend
their title the trails were phenomenal I
mean but it's hard to argue when you got
a blue sky day I mean everything looks
pretty good but this show is really fun
some challenging Hills with skiers
continuing to make history each year the
tradition of the Minnesota Finlandia
will continue for years to come with
this week's North Woods adventure in
Bemidji Josh Peterson Lakeland news a
beautiful day for that it was that was
before the snow we go quite a bit of
snow since that time if you enjoyed this
segment of Lakeland news please consider
making a tax-deductible contribution to
Lakeland PBS
you

videos.transcript:
the 41st Finlandia ski marathon in bichi
has been cancelled this year due to poor
skiing conditions the event was to be
held February 17th at bua Vista Ski Area
organizers cited lack of snow cover and
warm temperatures to come as the reason
for cancelling in a message posted to
their Facebook page organizers said that
the Minnesota Finlandia always strives
to provide a quality race that is
enjoyable and safe the message said that
decisions to cancel are never easy to
make make and come with great sadness
and that they're truly sorry to make
this decision but hope to see everyone
next year at the Minnesota Finlandia
2025 Lakeland news is member supported
content please consider supporting
Lakeland news
today

videos.transcript:
for the second year in a row the
Finlandia ski marathon in beiji has been
cancelled due to poor skiing conditions
the event was to be held February 15th
at buav Vista Ski Area organizers cited
lack of snow cover and uneven coverage
over most of the ski trails used for the
race is the reason for cancelling in a
press release organizers said they found
that despite the recent snowstorm much
of the snow was blown away by high winds
that Rob their Open Fields and hilltops
of the snow they'd need to connect the
sections that are unused
they say that without the ability to
connect those Trail sections that are
unusable they are unable to host a race
that meets their standards for safety
and a quality ski experience skiers who
would like to tour and ski the Westside
5K at bua Vista will be welcomed with
Race Day Grooming but there will be no
timing or Awards Lakeland news is a
nonprofit news organization donate to
Lakeland PBS today to support your local
news team

videos.title: Finlandia Cross-Country Ski Marathon Set To Begin
videos.transcript:
if you're in the mood for skiing this
weekend the Finlandia ski marathon is
the thing to do the CrossCountry ski
Marathon welcomes both beginners and
expert skiers and consists of five races
skiers from all over the country are
expected to participate including Canada
for the spectator sport it can take
anywhere from 1 to three hours to
complete the race and participants go
around the course two to three times
this year three there will be some
changes to the
course we decided to Short in the course
this year um it's normally a 25k Loop um
and we've gone down to an 11 11k across
the road here or what we call the west
side of buav Vista Ski Area now due to
the warm weather the start times for the
race have been moved up by 1 hour and
will now begin at 9:00 a.m. if you've
enjoyed this segment of Lakeland news
please consider making a tax deductible
contribution to Lakeland Public
Television

videos.title: Finlandia Ski Marathon (Finlandia-hiihto) 2016 - 32 km
videos.description: Start of Finlandia Ski Marathon 2016 classic 32 km. Why not participate as a skier or spectator in 2017 and stay a week in Lahti, Finland, for activity holidays with My Finland? This is a race for all skier levels - if you think 32 km is not too much - or too little :) . Book with My Finland ! Demande d'offre avev My Finland : www.myfinland.info .

videos.title: 39th Annual Finlandia Ski Marathon Cancelled
videos.description: Originally aired February 16, 2021 on Lakeland News
videos.transcript:
the 39th annual finlandia ski marathon
has been canceled
the event was scheduled to be held on
february 20th just north of bemidji
but event organizers say covid concerns
and
low snow cover made the decision a
difficult one

easy

the safety of skiers and volunteers
